message:
chore(beads): add bd-qz9j in-progress task for browser fail-closed coverage expansion

Track new task to expand browser fail-closed test coverage to
server/grpc/messaging profiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-o1au","title":"Fix fault injection tests after runtime_reset hook clearing","description":"The fault injection unit lane fails because test_fault_injection seals the allocator after calling asx_runtime_reset, but runtime_reset intentionally clears installed hooks. Added to Makefile and asx_runtime_reset. 18 unit tests all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-reg6","title":"EPIC: Rust Reference Fixture Capture Pipeline","description":"## Goal
Populate fixtures/rust_reference/ with golden outputs captured from the pinned Rust baseline
(commit 38c152405bd03e2bd9eecf178bfbbe9472fed861, toolchain nightly-2026-02-19).

## Why This Matters
The conformance gate currently runs against the C implementation's own fixtures, which validates
internal consistency but NOT Rust-vs-C behavioral parity. Without Rust-captured reference fixtures,
we cannot prove the C port produces identical semantic outputs for identical inputs. This is the
single highest-value remaining gap — it converts "self-consistent" into "provably equivalent."

## What "Done" Looks Like
- fixtures/rust_reference/ contains captured golden outputs for all 14 E2E fixture families (85+ scenarios)
- Each fixture has full provenance metadata (rust_baseline_commit, toolchain_commit_hash, cargo_lock_sha256)
- make conformance --fixtures-root fixtures/rust_reference passes with 0 semantic deltas
- Fixture schema validation passes against schemas/canonical_fixture.schema.json

## Architectural Context
The conformance infrastructure (tools/ci/run_conformance.sh) already supports --fixtures-root pointing
at Rust-captured data. The fixture schema, provenance map (docs/SOURCE_TO_FIXTURE_PROVENANCE_MAP.md,
28 provenance rows), and semantic delta budget policy (docs/SEMANTIC_DELTA_BUDGET.md, budget=0) are
all in place. This epic is purely about running the Rust side and capturing output.

## Risk
- The pinned Rust baseline may not build cleanly on current nightly (last verified 2026-02-26)
- Cargo.lock SHA must match exactly: 77f4bfaedfe5b8572dfca3181119f9432825ea3d868ee8f5d2793a6c5cdeffdc
- Any Rust-vs-C semantic delta discovered here would be a real bug (in either direction)","notes":"## IMPORTANT: FIXTURE DIRECTORY IS NOT EMPTY (2026-03-12 audit)

The fixtures/rust_reference/ directory IS populated with 7 families:
- smoke/ (10 fixtures), core_budget/, core_cancel/, core_channel/,
  core_lifecycle/, core_timer/, deployment_hardening/

HOWEVER, these are PLACEHOLDER/SCAFFOLD fixtures generated by the C implementation:
- semantic_digest values are "sha256:aaaaaaa..." (all-a placeholder)
- expected_events are empty arrays
- Provenance fields reference the correct Rust baseline but fixtures were NOT
  generated by actually running the Rust binary

The FEATURE_PARITY_VERIFICATION report (Gap #1) is correct: no actual Rust-generated
fixtures exist. The directory contains structural scaffolds.

## What This Epic Does
Replace placeholder fixtures with REAL Rust-generated golden outputs by:
1. Setting up Rust build environment
2. Building the Rust binary in fixture capture mode
3. Running it to generate real fixtures (replacing placeholders)
4. Validating the real fixtures against the schema
5. Running C conformance against real Rust fixtures (first true parity test)
6. E2E test verifying the entire pipeline

## Dependency Structure
Linear: .1 → .2 → .3 → .4 → .5, then .6 (e2e test) depends on .5

## Cross-Epic Dependencies
- bd-rkql depends on bd-reg6 (fuzzing needs Rust binary)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":600,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:05:50.816361571Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:40:12.333091287Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:40:12.331096181Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","phase3"]}
 {"id":"bd-reg6.1","title":"Set up Rust baseline build environment","description":"## Goal
Install the exact pinned Rust toolchain and check out the pinned baseline commit so that
the Rust reference implementation can be built reproducibly.

## Detailed Steps
1. Install rustup if not present
2. Install exact toolchain: nightly-2026-02-19 (compiler hash 7f99507f57e6c4aa0dce3daf6a13cca8cd4dd312)
3. Clone or fetch https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync
4. Check out pinned commit: 38c152405bd03e2bd9eecf178bfbbe9472fed861
5. Verify Cargo.lock SHA256 matches: 77f4bfaedfe5b8572dfca3181119f9432825ea3d868ee8f5d2793a6c5cdeffdc
6. Run cargo build --release to verify the baseline compiles
7. Run cargo test --release to verify the baseline's own tests pass

## Why This Specific Pinning
The entire conformance infrastructure is built around this exact commit and toolchain.
The provenance fields in every fixture (rust_baseline_commit, rust_toolchain_commit_hash,
cargo_lock_sha256) must match exactly, or the conformance runner will reject fixtures as
having mismatched provenance. See docs/RUST_BASELINE_PROVENANCE.md for the dual-baseline
rebase protocol.

## Acceptance Criteria
- cargo build --release succeeds on the pinned commit with the pinned toolchain
- cargo test --release passes with zero failures
- Cargo.lock SHA matches the documented value
- rustc --version reports the expected nightly version

## Testing
- Verify: sha256sum Cargo.lock matches expected hash
- Verify: rustc +nightly-2026-02-19 --version prints expected output
- Verify: cargo test --release 2>&1 | tail -5 shows "test result: ok"
- Document: number of passing tests in Rust baseline (for future reference)

## Why cargo test Matters
If the Rust baseline's own tests fail on the pinned commit, it means either the commit
is broken or the toolchain version is wrong. This is a pre-condition for generating
trustworthy reference fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:06:51.034288195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:47:00.272564103Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T20:47:00.270840933Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-reg6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-reg6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:06:51.034288195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
